abstract: Despite the increasing deployment of virtual streamers in e-commerce livestreaming, their effectiveness remains inconsistent. We explore this issue through the lens of intelligent interactivity, focusing on how and why the interaction effect between responsiveness and response naturalness shapes downstream outcomes in virtual livestreaming. We further test whether the presence of a livestream assistant moderates the effect of responsiveness under low response naturalness. One recall study and five experiments show that virtual streamers’ high responsiveness results in a higher likelihood of purchase intention and livestreaming continuance when response naturalness is high but nonexistent when naturalness is low. This joint effect can be explained by dual social influence processes: mental imagery quality and consumer-virtual streamer identification. Furthermore, the effect of responsiveness on both purchase intention and livestreaming continuance via mental imagery quality is only evident when a human livestream assistant is present in the low-naturalness context. Our findings contribute to the emerging field of virtual livestreaming and digital humans’ interactivity design. We also provide practical insights to e-commerce marketers and virtual streamer developers on how to optimize virtual livestreaming effectiveness.

ur.ai_reason: AI虚拟主播消费者购买意向实验
ur.tldr: 研究虚拟主播在电商直播中的有效性机制。通过回忆研究和多实验设计，考察响应性与回应自然性的交互效应，发现高响应性需配合高自然性才能提升购买意愿和直播继续意愿，否则无显著作用。心理意象质量和消费者-虚拟主播认同起中介作用，人类直播助手可弥补低自然性的不足，为虚拟主播设计优化提供理论依据。

title: EXPRESS: Toward Intelligent Virtual Streamers: Leveraging Responsiveness and Response Naturalness in E-commerce Livestreaming
title_zh: EXPRESS：迈向智能虚拟主播：利用响应能力与回应自然度提升电商直播
